entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Medium duration pigeonpeas (Cajanus cajan) grown in Peninsular India

entities.name: PENINSULAR INDIA
entities.description: Region in India where the study was conducted

entities.name: MONSOON
entities.description: Seasonal weather pattern that determines the sowing time of pigeonpeas

entities.name: RATOONING
entities.description: A harvesting method where plants are cut down to encourage a second flush of pods

entities.name: PICKING THE PODS
entities.description: A harvesting method where pods are manually picked

entities.name: SECOND HARVEST YIELD
entities.description: The yield obtained from the second flush of pods maturing around March

entities.name: FIRST HARVEST YIELD
entities.description: The yield obtained from the initial harvest maturing around December

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTER
entities.description: The research center where the experiments were conducted

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: A type of soil characterized by high water-holding capacity and soil cracking

entities.name: ALFISOLS
entities.description: A type of soil used in the experiments

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of water to the crops to increase yield

entities.name: WATER-HOLDING CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to retain water, which is higher in Vertisols

entities.name: SOIL CRACKING
entities.description: The process of soil splitting in Vertisols which can damage root systems

entities.name: ROOT SYSTEM
entities.description: The underground part of the pigeonpea plant

entities.name: POD SHATTERING
entities.description: The loss of seeds due to pods breaking open
